meta.page_title: Inner Child Test - Heal Your Childhood Wounds | DopaBrain
meta.description: What childhood wound shapes your adult life? Discover your inner child archetype: Abandoned, Criticized, Invisible, Parentified, Overprotected, or Playful. 8 questions, free.
meta.keywords: inner child test, inner child quiz, childhood wound test, inner child healing, inner child archetype, childhood trauma test, reparenting quiz, inner child work
meta.og_title: Inner Child Test - Heal Your Childhood Wounds
meta.og_description: 8 questions reveal your inner child archetype and personalized healing affirmations.
meta.twitter_title: Inner Child Test - Heal Your Childhood Wounds
meta.twitter_description: 8 questions reveal your inner child archetype!

intro.hook: What childhood wound still shapes your adult life?
intro.title: Inner Child Test
intro.subtitle: Heal Your Childhood Wounds

question.0: When someone you care about doesn't text back for a long time, your first thought is?
question.0a: They're probably going to leave me — I should reach out first
question.0b: I probably did something wrong to upset them
question.0c: It's fine, I don't want to bother them anyway
question.0d: I should check if they're okay — they might need help
question.0e: Something bad must have happened — I feel anxious
question.0f: They're probably busy — I'll do something fun meanwhile
question.1: When you receive a genuine compliment, you typically:
question.1a: Feel suspicious — they probably want something from me
question.1b: Immediately think of ways I could be even better
question.1c: Deflect it — 'oh, it was nothing really'
question.1d: Thank them but feel uncomfortable being the focus
question.1e: Feel overwhelmed and unsure how to respond
question.1f: Accept it warmly and genuinely feel good about it
question.2: On a rare free day with absolutely no responsibilities:
question.2a: Feel anxious being alone and reach out to someone
question.2b: Use it for self-improvement or catching up on tasks
question.2c: Ask others what they'd like to do
question.2d: Catch up on things I've been putting off for others
question.2e: Stay home where it's safe and comfortable
question.2f: Do something spontaneous and creative just for fun
question.3: When you make a noticeable mistake at work or school:
question.3a: Fear everyone will see me differently and pull away
question.3b: Beat myself up and replay the moment endlessly
question.3c: Apologize profusely even if it was minor
question.3d: Quickly fix it and everyone else's problems too
question.3e: Feel paralyzed and afraid to try again
question.3f: Laugh it off, learn from it, and move forward
question.4: In close relationships, your deepest fear is:
question.4a: Being abandoned or replaced by someone better
question.4b: Not being good enough for the other person
question.4c: Being a burden or taking up too much space
question.4d: Letting people down or failing to protect them
question.4e: Being hurt or the relationship feeling unsafe
question.4f: Losing the joy and playfulness in the connection
question.5: How do you typically handle your own emotional needs?
question.5a: I look to others to meet them — being alone feels scary
question.5b: I push through — having needs feels like weakness
question.5c: I minimize them — other people's needs matter more
question.5d: I take care of everyone else first, then maybe myself
question.5e: I meet them in controlled, predictable ways only
question.5f: I listen to what I need and honor it naturally
question.6: When conflict arises in a close relationship:
question.6a: Panic and do anything to prevent them from leaving
question.6b: Assume it's my fault and try to be perfect going forward
question.6c: Go quiet and withdraw to keep the peace
question.6d: Try to mediate and fix everything for everyone
question.6e: Freeze up and want to retreat to safety
question.6f: Address it honestly and with calm confidence
question.7: Late at night, the thought that visits you most often is:
question.7a: Am I truly loved, or will everyone eventually leave?
question.7b: I'm still not enough — I need to try harder
question.7c: Does anyone really see the real me?
question.7d: I'm exhausted from carrying everyone else's weight
question.7e: The world feels overwhelming and unsafe
question.7f: Life is an adventure and I'm growing every day

result.percentile: Only {pct} of people have the {type} archetype

abandonedChild.name: The Abandoned Child
abandonedChild.tagline: A heart that learned love means eventually being left
abandonedChild.description: Your inner child carries the wound of abandonment. An early experience of loss, absence, or emotional unavailability taught you that love is unreliable. You may cling to relationships, seek constant reassurance, or preemptively leave before being left. Your healing begins when you learn that your worth isn't determined by someone else's ability to stay.
abandonedChild.trait1: Fear of being left or replaced
abandonedChild.prompt: Dear little one, you are not too much. You were never the reason anyone left. You deserve love that stays.

criticizedChild.name: The Criticized Child
criticizedChild.tagline: A soul that learned love must be earned through perfection
criticizedChild.description: Your inner child carries the wound of criticism. Growing up, approval was conditional — tied to achievement, behavior, or meeting impossible standards. You developed a harsh inner critic that drives perfectionism and self-doubt. Your healing begins when you realize you were always enough, even before you accomplished anything.
criticizedChild.prompt: Dear little one, you don't have to be perfect to be loved. You were always enough, exactly as you are.

invisibleChild.name: The Invisible Child
invisibleChild.tagline: A presence that learned to take up as little space as possible
invisibleChild.description: Your inner child carries the wound of invisibility. You learned that your needs, feelings, and opinions didn't matter — or that expressing them caused problems. You became a master at reading rooms, pleasing others, and disappearing. Your healing begins when you practice taking up space unapologetically.
invisibleChild.prompt: Dear little one, you are allowed to be seen. Your voice matters. You don't have to shrink to be safe.

parentifiedChild.name: The Parentified Child
parentifiedChild.tagline: A child who grew up too fast and forgot how to play
parentifiedChild.description: Your inner child carries the wound of lost childhood. You became the caretaker — emotionally or practically — for your family too early. You learned that being responsible, mature, and selfless was the way to survive. Your healing begins when you give yourself permission to be taken care of, to play, and to rest.
parentifiedChild.prompt: Dear little one, it was never your job to carry the family. You're allowed to put the weight down and just be a kid.

overprotectedChild.name: The Overprotected Child
overprotectedChild.tagline: A spirit wrapped in safety that became a cage
overprotectedChild.description: Your inner child carries the wound of overprotection. While your caregivers meant well, the constant shielding taught you that the world is dangerous and you can't handle it. You struggle with risk, independence, and trusting your own strength. Your healing begins when you take small brave steps and prove to yourself that you are capable.
overprotectedChild.prompt: Dear little one, the world isn't as scary as they told you. You are braver and stronger than you know.

playfulChild.name: The Playful Child
playfulChild.tagline: A heart that remembers how to wonder, create, and laugh
playfulChild.description: Your inner child is remarkably connected and alive. You've maintained (or reconnected with) your capacity for joy, spontaneity, creativity, and genuine self-expression. This doesn't mean you haven't faced challenges — it means you've found ways to honor your inner child's need for play and authenticity. Keep nurturing this gift.
playfulChild.prompt: Dear inner child, thank you for staying alive in me. I promise to keep playing, creating, and laughing with you.

share.text: My inner child archetype is '{type}'! What's yours?
